statement: The UK voted to leave the EU in June 2016 (51.9% Leave) and formally departed on 31 January 2020. The UK Office for Budget Responsibility estimated in 2023 that Brexit would reduce long-run UK GDP by approximately 4% compared to remaining in the EU, primarily through reduced trade intensity.
source_name: UK Office for Budget Responsibility — Fiscal Risks and Sustainability Report 2023
caveats: Brexit's full economic impact is difficult to isolate from COVID-19 effects and other factors. The UK's departure from a large existing trading bloc is a fundamentally different situation from Iceland potentially joining the EU.

related_entries.statement: Article 50 of the Treaty on European Union (TEU) provides that any member state may decide to withdraw from the Union in accordance with its own constitutional requirements. The withdrawal process involves a two-year negotiation period, extendable by unanimous agreement of the European Council.

Under the UK-EU Trade and Cooperation Agreement (2020), the UK regained the status of an independent coastal state with sovereign rights over its EEZ. The agreement included a 5.5-year transition period (2021–2026) with EU fishing access gradually reduced by 25%. Post-transition, annual negotiations determine mutual access and quota sharing.

citations.report_title: Engin fordæmi fyrir leið Íslands í átt að ESB

claims.claim_text: Útganga Bretlands úr ESB (Brexit) var meginbreytingin sem átti sér stað í Evrópusambandinu á undanförnum árum.
ESB hefur tekist að rétta úr kútnum eftir útgöngu Bretlands.
Eftir Brexit þéttu ESB-ríkin sem eftir urðu sínar raðir í stað þess að fleiri ríki segðu sig úr.
Óttinn var að fleiri ríki fylgdu Bretlandi úr ESB eftir Brexit, en það gerðist ekki.
Engin umræða er nú um að einhvert ESB-ríki ætli að segja sig úr sambandinu.
Umræða er í Bretlandi um að útgangan hafi verið mistök.

citations.report_title: Snorri segir húsnæði verða jafndýrt þótt vextir lækki

claims.claim_text: Brexit hafi verið mjög erfitt fyrir Breta og þeir séu enn að glíma við afleiðingar þess.
Ekki sé auðvelt að yfirgefa Evrópusambandið þegar í það er komið.
